compiled_state.description:
# Publish governance: GOVERNANCE.md, MAINTAINERS.md, CoC contact fix (CODEOWNERS explicitly skipped)

## Why

Governance is implicit today; an outsider cannot see who decides, how maintainers are added, or where a Code of Conduct report goes if it concerns the maintainer himself. Peer convergence (curl's GOVERNANCE.md https://github.com/curl/curl/blob/master/docs/GOVERNANCE.md; the CNCF small-project template https://github.com/cncf/project-template/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md; opensource.guide) gives a one-page recipe. The one thing no template covers — and the thing readers of an org-backed solo project probe — is the Nava-PBC-backed-but-individual-led ambiguity, so it gets its own section.

## What

- `GOVERNANCE.md` (~1 page) with the seven convergent sections:
  1. Overview — maintainer-led, created and stewarded at Nava PBC; names the current lead maintainer; one-line reorientation: review happens on Gerrit, GitHub is a read-only mirror.
  2. Roles — contributor / maintainer / lead maintainer, with rebar-specific powers: Gerrit submit rights (Contributors group), ticket-store write access, release authority.
  3. Decision-making — consensus among maintainers; lead maintainer has final say (curl's model in role language); routine decisions happen in Gerrit review and rebar tickets; ADRs under `docs/adr/` record architectural choices.
  4. Becoming a maintainer — sustained quality contributions through Gerrit review ("a few quality changes merged as proof" — curl's bar), invited by the lead maintainer.
  5. Stepping down / emeritus — inactivity → access removed for security reasons, non-punitive, listed as emeritus.
  6. Nava PBC's role — what the org provides (Gerrit host, maintainer employment) and does not control (contributors participate as individuals — CNCF framing). Includes the succession decision (see open decisions).
  7. Changing this document — via a Gerrit change, lead maintainer approves.
- `MAINTAINERS.md`: table (name, GitHub handle, Gerrit username, scope, affiliation), one row today; "Emeritus maintainers" placeholder section; "changes are proposed as a Gerrit change against this file — see GOVERNANCE.md". Separate file despite one row (health-file scanners look for it by name).
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`: keep the Contributor Covenant; change the enforcement contact to role-based with a Nava backstop and add: "if a report concerns the lead maintainer, contact <Nava backstop> instead." **Also update the matching contact line in `SUPPORT.md` (~line 31 hard-codes hello@navapbc.com as the CoC contact)** so the two files never disagree.
- Assess CC 2.1 → 3.0 (3.0 is the 2026 default — Django/Mastodon/Hanami adopted; https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/3/0/code_of_conduct/); record the upgrade decision.
- Decision persistence (in-repo, not ticket-only): record the three design decisions — lead-maintainer-vs-BDFL language; CC version; CODEOWNERS non-goal — in ADR `docs/adr/0038-governance-artifacts.md` (one ADR, three short sections), with a ticket comment pointing at it.
- CODEOWNERS non-goal rationale (tense-correct: the ruleset exists today; the bot is sibling-story work, not yet live): GitHub PRs cannot merge here (a repository ruleset rejects PR merges today; story nonsecular-arthritic-sidewinder additionally adds an auto-answer bot) — CODEOWNERS only auto-requests reviewers and gates merges on PRs, so it would be dead config falsely implying PR review. Gerrit's code-owners plugin likewise skipped (one human owns everything).
- Open org decisions to resolve with Nava before close (record answers in GOVERNANCE.md §6 + the ADR): (a) succession — whether project authority (PyPI name `nava-rebar`, the Gerrit host, the GitHub org repo) rests with the person or with Nava PBC; (b) the named Nava CoC backstop contact.

## Scope

IN: GOVERNANCE.md, MAINTAINERS.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md contact + version assessment, SUPPORT.md contact-line sync, ADR 0038.
OUT: CODEOWNERS (either flavor); formal voting machinery; a foundation-style CoC committee; the rest of SUPPORT.md (owned by the intake story).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GOVERNANCE.md present with all seven sections — prove: grep for section headings incl. "Nava" and "emeritus"; Gerrit-mirror reorientation in the first ~10 lines.
- [ ] MAINTAINERS.md present — prove: `grep -qi "emeritus" MAINTAINERS.md` and the table has ≥1 row + change-process pointer.
- [ ] CoC enforcement contact is role-based with a backstop and the maintainer-concerning-report escape — prove: `grep -q "lead maintainer" CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` and the contact address appears identically in CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md and SUPPORT.md (extract and diff the two address strings). If Nava has not yet designated a named backstop at execution time, the doc ships with hello@navapbc.com as the backstop address and GOVERNANCE.md §6 notes the designation is pending — that interim wording SATISFIES this AC (the org answer upgrades it later; not a blocker).
- [ ] ADR 0038 committed with the three decisions + rationale — prove: `test -f docs/adr/0038-governance-artifacts.md`.
- [ ] GOVERNANCE.md §6 (Nava's role) is present and non-empty in all cases — prove: `grep -qi "succession" GOVERNANCE.md`. It contains either the org's answers (authority/PyPI/Gerrit-host ownership; named backstop) or an explicit "pending designation" sentence naming the interim arrangement — both forms satisfy this AC; silent omission does not.
compiled_state.title: Publish governance: GOVERNANCE.md, MAINTAINERS.md, CoC contact fix (no CODEOWNERS)
